notes: ROOT CAUSE: rice's real T-DNA Chr3:16,439,674 has 5'+3' clips that hit AF234315.1 vector backbone at 100%/53bp and 100%/67bp. s04b contigs DO cover this region (NODE_1 covers AF234315.1:6222-6323 at 100%/33bp), but classify_site_tiers' bitscore-best merge in Phase 1.5 prefers the primary transgene_db univec hit over the extra_transgene_db element_db hit when bitscores tie (equal identity, roughly equal aln_len). Condition is strict '>' not '>=', so first hit wins by iteration order. Result: transgene_positive=False (source=univec). After running step 4b first (122 contigs) and step 5 with --extra-element-db, Phase 1.5 now retains 48 transgene-positive sites but Chr3:16,439,674 is still excluded. Fix requires policy change: prefer any element_db hit (primary OR extra) over univec, regardless of bitscore. Out of scope for Task 7 -- recommend a separate Task 10.

A2_3 step 5 was killed after ~60 min (assembly phase processing 3-4 of 27 positive sites). Positive signal: Phase 1 VALIDATED SLM_r2.0ch01:91,002,744 high/element=yes with 3p=NODE_1_length_4160_cov_7.260103 from s04b contigs at 100%/124bp -- confirming --extra-element-db works when the contig match is STRICTLY LONGER than any transgene_db hit (124bp beats 76bp univec, bitscore actually higher). Mechanism validates where rice's 33bp contig tie-by-bitscore does not. ch01_91002744 was queued 3rd; no Phase 4 verdict written.
